title: Doctrine text stops misinforming Codex sessions, with the counts kept straight
action: Per D1/D2/D21/D22/D24/D25. FOUR COUNTS EXIST AND THEY ARE ALL TRUE OF DIFFERENT THINGS — measure before you touch any of them: `ls hooks/bee-*.mjs` = 9 hook SCRIPTS; `Object.keys(require('./.codex/hooks.json').hooks).length` = 8 Codex LIFECYCLE EVENTS; the same expression on hooks/claude-hooks.json = 7 Claude lifecycle events; `Object.keys(require('./.bee/config.json').hooks).length` = 6 CONFIG TOGGLES, which test_onboard_bee.mjs:457 asserts green. A prose claim of 'six' is CORRECT when it means the toggles (docs/06-runtime-integration.md:120 'all six hooks default-on, each toggleable' is true — leave it). Fix only claims where the number is wrong for the quantity named. STEP 1 — rewrite the guardrails sentence at skills/bee-hive/templates/AGENTS.block.md:80 to state exactly three things: both runtimes ship hooks rendered from one shared catalog (.codex/hooks.json, 8 lifecycle events, onboarding-tracked at .bee/onboarding.json:51); the enforcement floor is the shared helpers, identical on both; and Codex hook EXECUTION is unverified, so self-honoring remains the fallback on any runtime whose hook execution is unconfirmed. STEP 2 — correct the stale corners: docs/02-architecture.md:219, :221, :223; README.md:312, :444 ('7 lifecycle events' -> 8), :479; INSTALL.md:63; docs/01-distillation.md:36 and :111 ('exactly 6 hooks wrapping shared lib/ modules'); docs/05-roadmap.md:69; docs/decisions/0002-scribing-skill.md:12 ('The 6-hook cap is unchanged'); docs/06-runtime-integration.md:40 and :5-6 (the 'Claude Code gets a hook-driven skeleton / Codex gets the helper-enforced skeleton' framing that survives the sentence fix verbatim); docs/07-contracts.md:104 and :216. STEP 3 — regen chain, in this order (D24): `node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs`, then `node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply`, then `node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write`. The manifest hashes skills/** and hooks/** (release_manifest.mjs:131,133), so editing the template moves release_manifest --check; onboard --apply does NOT render the two plugin trees, only render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs does, so rendering must precede the manifest write or stale trees get frozen as authoritative. STEP 4 — scripts/test_agents_budget.mjs hard-fails at 20480 bytes; AGENTS.md measured 19181 before this change. If the rewrite pushes past it, tighten the new wording.

truths: The retired sentence appears in zero live copies: the template, all four projections, and the merged root AGENTS.md.
The replacement preserves the unverified-execution caveat — no claim of proven Codex hook parity.
Every corrected count matches its own quantity, and the TRUE six-toggle claims are left intact.
AGENTS.md stays under 20480 bytes, achieved by tightening wording and never by raising the threshold.
release_manifest --check is green at cap time.
artifacts: Rewritten doctrine sentence, corrected corners, regenerated projections and plugin trees, refreshed manifest and ledger
prohibitions: Never edit AGENTS.md's bee block by hand as the primary fix — it is merged from the template.
Never raise HARD_FAIL_BYTES or WARN_BYTES in scripts/test_agents_budget.mjs.
Never 'fix' docs/06-runtime-integration.md:120's six-toggle claim — it is true.
Do not touch docs/history/** — archived reports are not live copies.

verify: node scripts/test_agents_budget.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/run_verify.mjs

trace.outcome: Doctrine sentence rewritten per D2 (shared catalog / identical helper floor / unverified Codex hook execution) in the canonical template and propagated to all four projections plus merged AGENTS.md; every live stale hook-count and Claude-first framing corrected against its own quantity (9 scripts, 8 Codex events, 7 Claude events), the six config-toggle claims left intact; regen chain run render->onboard--apply->manifest--write in D24 order.
trace.friction: scripts/test_store_lock.mjs case (c) recorded 2 mutual-exclusion violations on the first full run_verify at concurrency=5, then passed all cases (a)-(h) in isolation and on the full-chain re-run. Load-sensitive flake in the stale-lock racer case, unrelated to this cell's markdown/manifest diff; worth a stability look before it costs another worker a red baseline.
